marketState: Neutral: near-term overbought/rich-to-fair-value risk offset by modestly bullish seasonality and weather trend
summary: NG=F trades at $3.245, +3.3pp above its seasonal median and $0.19-$0.32 over modeled fair value across every horizon (now through +21D), though comfortably inside each fair-value band rather than at an extreme. Weather demand is running well above 5-year normal both near-term (+8.7%) and in the 8-16 day window (+11%, rangePos 0.74), supportive of cooling-season gas burn, but the 24h demand trend is already softening (-7.7). Short-term technicals are stretched (RSI 74-84 and price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h), and storage posted a bearish surprise (+13 Bcf vs forecast, +175 Bcf surplus vs 5y), reinforcing near-term downside/pullback risk. Managed money is net short near the 18th percentile of its 5-year range (mild contrarian upside risk on short-covering), and the futures curve shows a large winter contango (Dec/Jan near $3.95-$4.37) confirming seasonal strength is already priced further out. Net take: near-term mean-reversion risk from overbought/over-fair conditions, but medium-term seasonality, weather trend, and a rising fair-value glidepath (from $2.93 now to $3.06 by +21D) keep the longer-term bias cautiously long, albeit with explicitly low seasonality reliability.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +11% vs 5y with rangePos 0.74, supporting above-normal cooling-season burn; seasonality shows 63% historical odds of a higher price in 4 weeks with a +2.3% typical move; and the fair-value glidepath rises from $2.929 now to $3.057 by +21D, narrowing today's overvaluation as time passes rather than requiring price to fall.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained break below $2.85 (below the now fair-value band low), 8-16D weather demand reverting to/below 5y normal, or continued larger-than-forecast storage builds widening the +175 Bcf surplus

shortTerm.rationale: Price is stretched on short-term technicals (RSI 74-84, trading above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h) while sitting $0.32 over 'now' fair value; the front-vs-next backwardation (-$0.103) and the bearish +13 Bcf storage surprise both add fundamental weight to a near-term pullback toward fair value.
shortTerm.invalidation: Close and hold above $3.43 (top of the now fair-value band), or 1-7D weather demand reaccelerating back above the recent +8.7% vs 5y pace

keyDrivers: Weather demand +8.7%/+11% vs 5y (1-7D/8-16D) supports cooling-season gas burn
Price sits $0.19-$0.32 over fair value at every horizon, but fair value itself rises into +21D
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise
Managed-money net short at just the 18th 5y percentile - some short-covering upside risk
Short-term technicals (5m/15m/1h) overbought, trading above the upper Bollinger band
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly LOW (51pp 10-90% spread) - wide dispersion of outcomes
Large and growing storage surplus keeps structural bearish pressure on price
1-7D weather demand trend is already decelerating (-7.7 in 24h), could remove the near-term tailwind
